domains.title: Core CLI and Git behavior
domains.objective: Verify that every public command, Git-state decision, configuration source, and hook lifecycle path remains correct, advisory-first, and safe for user work.
scope: CLI commands, options, exit codes, human output, and JSON output
Git discovery, repository shapes, configuration precedence, and protected branches
Native and manager-composed hook install, repair, ownership, and removal
Safe staged fixes, commit amendment, secret scanning, and push-range selection
procedure: Inventory the current public commands, options, config keys, hooks, and exit contracts
Exercise empty, unborn, detached, bare, shallow, worktree, submodule, and multi-remote states
Exercise invalid arguments, failed Git calls, uninspectable paths, partial setup, repeated setup, repair, and removal
Compare observed behavior with configuration, external-interface, and lifecycle documentation
File every unresolved defect or unsafe ambiguity as a separate finding
requiredEvidence: Dated focused test results for every changed or suspect command path
Full npm test and packed npm lifecycle results
Command-to-documentation mapping and any explicit unsupported boundary
Links to findings, fixes, regression tests, and merged evidence commits
passCriteria: Every public command and option has an evidence-backed behavior mapping
Required repository and lifecycle edge cases are exercised meaningfully
Confirmed defects have regression tests and the smallest reliable fixes
Observable behavior, exit codes, and limitations agree with canonical documentation

cadence.normal: Every full RHR; continuously through required CI for automated checks
triggers: New command, configuration key, hook, Git-state mutation, or integration manager
Change to setup, repair, uninstall, safe-fix, amend, or protected-branch behavior
Supported Node.js, Git, repository-shape, or process-execution boundary changes

continuousEvidence: npm test
npm run test:lifecycle:npm
Required CI OS and Node.js matrix
manualEvidence: Judgment about safe mutation, advisory defaults, and ambiguous ownership
Reconciliation of public contracts with current behavior

domains.title: Security, secrets, paths, and subprocesses
domains.objective: Verify trust boundaries and failure behavior for untrusted repository content, paths, Git output, configuration, environment, local tools, workflows, and release credentials.
scope: Subprocess argv, environment, cwd, timeouts, signals, and cleanup
Secret-diff parsing, staged-versus-unstaged separation, and enforcement decisions
Path traversal, symlinks, permissions, temporary files, and ownership checks
Dependencies, workflow permissions, action pins, fork safety, tokens, and supply-chain controls
procedure: Refresh the asset, actor, input, and trust-boundary inventory
Review every changed spawn and file operation for shell-free fixed-argv and fail-safe behavior
Exercise adversarial paths, malformed or failed Git output, binary data, Unicode, and secret edge cases
Review dependency advisories and live security controls without exposing vulnerability details publicly
File each unresolved risk with severity, impact, owner, and disposition
requiredEvidence: Threat-boundary map tied to current code and workflows
Regression results for every repaired vulnerability or bypass
npm audit, CodeQL, secret-scanning, and relevant live-setting evidence
Residual-risk records with compensating controls and expiry where accepted
passCriteria: No known shell interpolation, unsafe path traversal, or silent security downgrade remains undispositioned
Staged and unstaged content remain separated and user work remains preserved
Security failures produce the documented advisory or blocking decision
Every Critical or High risk is fixed or explicitly blocks a green result

cadence.normal: Every full RHR; continuous dependency and code-scanning surveillance
triggers: Changes to process execution, parsing, paths, permissions, secrets, or local-tool resolution
Changes to workflow permissions, fork handling, tokens, releases, or repository security settings
New high-severity advisory, CodeQL alert, vulnerability report, or security boundary

continuousEvidence: npm audit --audit-level=high
Required CodeQL analysis and code-scanning ruleset
Secret, subprocess, path, and workflow-policy tests
manualEvidence: Read-back of live GitHub security settings and private-reporting controls

domains.title: Test quality and meaningful coverage
domains.objective: Verify that automated evidence exercises real behavior, fails for the defects it claims to catch, remains hermetic across platforms, and does not confuse 100% coverage with completeness.
scope: Runtime units, CLI subprocesses, real-Git fixtures, property tests, and lifecycle integration
Coverage scope, exclusions, suppressions, ownership, and badge freshness
Mocks, cleanup, order dependence, inherited environment, platform assumptions, and flaky processes
Changed-file selection, deletions, renames, generated evidence, and CI-only execution
procedure: Map every current runtime module and maintenance tool to meaningful test ownership
Review new and changed assertions for behavioral outcomes rather than incidental execution
Exercise deletion, rename, unusual-path, failure, cleanup, and inherited-environment boundaries
Run repeated or reordered groups when state leakage or flakiness is plausible
Confirm practical regressions fail against the defective baseline before accepting a fix
requiredEvidence: 100% line, branch, and function coverage results on the defined runtime denominator
Named test ownership for runtime and repository maintenance behavior
CI-equivalent COMMITMENT_ISSUES=0 test result
Flake, cleanup, platform, and coverage-suppression dispositions
passCriteria: Every production branch and documented critical scenario has a meaningful assertion or explicit manual owner
Coverage scope and exclusions remain exact, justified, and test-owned
No green-but-vacuous hook test, silent skip, state leak, or snapshot masking remains
Known evidence gaps are filed separately with an owner and target

cadence.normal: Every full RHR; required CI on every pull request
triggers: Changes to test helpers, coverage policy, CI test selection, sharding, or lifecycle fixtures
New runtime module, maintenance tool, platform lane, suppression, or conditional skip
Flaky, CI-only, performance, leaked-process, or false-positive test behavior

continuousEvidence: npm test
COMMITMENT_ISSUES=0 npm test
npm run test:coverage and coverage badge check
manualEvidence: Risk-based selection of repeated, randomized, or platform-specific runs

domains.title: Terminal UX, JSON output, and generated states
domains.objective: Verify that every human and machine-readable outcome is accurate, accessible, compact, stable, and actionable across terminal capabilities without hiding product decisions in styling.
scope: One-box human output, standard and fun tones, severity, quiet modes, and next-step wording
JSON schema, stdout and stderr separation, exit decisions, and compatibility
Color, NO_COLOR, redirection, width, Unicode, ASCII boundaries, and accessibility
Live message states, generated SVGs, terminal demo source, and visual fidelity
procedure: Inventory every public message and JSON state produced by current commands
Exercise narrow, color-disabled, redirected, malformed-terminal, and non-interactive cases
Validate structured output against the published schema and matching human decisions
Regenerate affected state assets from source and inspect rendered output
File wording, accessibility, schema, or visual drift as separate findings when not fixed immediately
requiredEvidence: Focused terminal, message, JSON, state-runner, and asset test results
Exact generated-asset equality or an explained reviewed visual change
Representative rendered output for any user-facing wording or layout change
Schema and documentation links for every new or changed public state
passCriteria: Human and JSON outcomes express the same status and enforcement decision
Every command renders at most one final box and remains usable without color
Malformed terminal dimensions and redirected output cannot crash or corrupt guidance
Generated evidence is current, accessible, and reviewed rather than threshold-forced

cadence.normal: Every full RHR; generated-state and schema checks on every relevant pull request
triggers: Changes to user-facing wording, tone transforms, JSON fields, exit semantics, or terminal rendering
New command state, generated message asset, demo scene, or supported terminal boundary
Reports of clipping, duplicate boxes, color inconsistency, or inaccessible output

continuousEvidence: Message, terminal, UI, JSON-schema, and message-state tests
Generated SVG byte-equality checks
Render demo metadata and visual-comparison workflow
manualEvidence: Visual inspection of changed output at realistic widths

domains.title: Package managers and cross-platform behavior
domains.objective: Verify the exact packed artifact through supported install, hook, workspace, upgrade, repair, and removal flows without claiming environments that lack evidence.
scope: npm, pnpm, Yarn Classic, Yarn Berry node-modules, and Bun lifecycles
Minimum and current Node.js lines across Linux, macOS, and Windows
POSIX shells, Fish, PowerShell, Command Prompt, IDEs, and Git clients
Root and nested workspaces, linked worktrees, reinstall, upgrade, downgrade, and uninstall
procedure: Build or identify one exact packed artifact and record its identity
Run supported package-manager lifecycle lanes without source-checkout leakage
Exercise workspace, worktree, repeated install, migration, repair, and uninstall ownership
Classify each claimed environment as CI-verified, manually verified, unverified, or unsupported
Narrow documentation or file a finding when evidence does not support a claim
requiredEvidence: Exact tarball identity and package-manager lifecycle results
OS, Node.js, shell, workspace, and Git-client evidence matrix
Preservation evidence for unrelated scripts, hooks, and project files
Explicit unverified and unsupported rows with follow-up ownership
passCriteria: Every officially supported package-manager mode installs and executes the packed runtime
Public compatibility claims do not exceed automated or documented manual evidence
Setup, repair, migration, and removal preserve unrelated project-owned state
Unavailable GUI or platform lanes are marked unverified rather than passed

cadence.normal: Every full RHR; weekly lifecycle drift checks and required compatibility CI
triggers: Supported Node.js, package-manager, operating-system, shell, workspace, or Git-client changes
Package lifecycle, hook launcher, migration, upgrade, or uninstall changes
Upstream package-manager or runner behavior that changes the tested matrix

continuousEvidence: Required OS, Node.js, package-manager, shell, and migration CI lanes
Weekly repository-health lifecycle lanes
Package and shell compatibility tests
manualEvidence: GUI Git-client and IDE-triggered Git operations

domains.title: CI/CD and GitHub Actions
domains.objective: Verify that workflows, required checks, dependency automation, and live repository controls prove distinct useful behavior with least privilege and fail closed.
scope: Workflow triggers, permissions, fork behavior, matrices, caches, artifacts, concurrency, and timeouts
Trusted change classification, DCO, quality, lifecycle, CodeQL, and CI Success aggregation
Dependabot, Scorecard, scheduled health, visual, and release automation
Live rulesets, required checks, review controls, code scanning, and security settings
procedure: Inventory every tracked workflow, action pin, automation config, and settings-owned control
Review untrusted expressions, checkout boundaries, permissions, credentials, caches, and artifacts
Verify every required dependency fails CI Success on skipped, cancelled, timed-out, or failed results
Read live rulesets and security settings without mutating shared infrastructure
File mismatches, weak smoke checks, redundant jobs, or unverified fork behavior separately
requiredEvidence: Workflow-to-support and behavior evidence matrix
actionlint, workflow-policy, required CI, CodeQL, and fork results
Live ruleset and security-control read-back
Documented disposition for every supplemental, skipped, or settings-owned control
passCriteria: The single CI Success context fails closed on every required dependency
Fork and pull-request execution cannot expose write tokens or trust proposed workflow code
Third-party actions and downloaded tools are immutably pinned and verified
Live required checks, review rules, code scanning, and documented governance agree

cadence.normal: Every full RHR; required CI and weekly or scheduled automation continuously
triggers: Workflow, action, matrix, required-check, ruleset, branch-protection, or Dependabot changes
Repository permission, ownership, secret, environment, or security-setting changes
CI bypass, fork-safety, cache, artifact, or required-status incident

continuousEvidence: Required CI Success graph and workflow-policy tests
Scheduled CodeQL, Scorecard, repository-health, and Dependabot automation
Actionlint and high-severity dependency audit
manualEvidence: Live ruleset, security-setting, and permission read-back
Judgment about distinct evidence, fork trust, and settings-owned automation

domains.title: Release, packaging, and upgrades
domains.objective: Verify that versioning, reviewed-mainline authority, the exact package artifact, trusted publishing, provenance, recovery, upgrades, and immutable release records agree.
scope: Version, changelog, release notes, tags, ancestry, GitHub Releases, npm, and provenance
Exact package allowlist, runtime imports, executable modes, links, licenses, size, and dependencies
Fresh packed installation, setup, hooks, repair, repeated init, upgrade, downgrade, and uninstall
Collision preflight, partial-publication classification, retry, rollback, and fix-forward rules
procedure: Run release metadata and collision preflight without creating or moving a tag
Inspect one exact package manifest and exercise that artifact in clean repositories
Verify tag authority, workflow permissions, provenance subject, registry metadata, and immutable assets
Exercise or review partial-publication and upgrade or downgrade recovery scenarios
File every identity, byte, provenance, or recovery mismatch as a release finding
requiredEvidence: Exact candidate name, version, source commit, hashes, integrity, sizes, files, and toolchain
Fresh packed lifecycle and package-content validation results
Tag ancestry, trusted-publishing, provenance, registry, and GitHub Release evidence
Explicit retry, rollback, or new-patch decision for any partial state
passCriteria: Version, tag, reviewed source, changelog, package, provenance, npm, and GitHub Release identify one candidate
Every runtime dependency and shipped Markdown link exists in the exact tarball
No consumed tag, published version, or immutable asset is moved, reused, overwritten, or unpublished
No Critical or High release-integrity finding remains open

cadence.normal: Every full RHR and before each release candidate is authorized
triggers: Version, tag, changelog, package contents, release workflow, provenance, or trusted-publisher changes
Dependency install lifecycle, upgrade, downgrade, retry, rollback, or recovery changes
Any partial publication, tag collision, artifact mismatch, or registry incident

continuousEvidence: Release metadata, mainline ancestry, package, and recovery tests
npm pack manifest and packed lifecycle checks
Tag-triggered trusted-publishing and provenance workflow
manualEvidence: Explicit release authorization and collision review
Post-publication registry, signature, provenance, asset, and recovery verification

domains.title: Documentation, governance, and promotional assets
domains.objective: Verify that public and maintainer material matches merged behavior and evidence, governance is actionable, and promotional assets show the real product without unsupported claims.
scope: README, changelog, user guides, contracts, audits, support, security, and release documentation
License, DCO, contribution policy, roles, sensitive access, continuity, and vulnerability reporting
Badges, links, examples, command output, support claims, and package documentation
Demo, before-and-after, Product Hunt media, source assets, accessibility, and rendering workflows
procedure: Inventory retained documents, community-health files, governance records, badges, links, and assets
Run documented commands where practical and compare output with current merged behavior
Reconcile support, security, privacy, compliance, governance, and release claims with evidence
Regenerate and visually inspect changed assets from their canonical source
File every contradiction, broken link, unsupported claim, or ownership gap separately
requiredEvidence: Document and community-health inventory with canonical ownership
Documentation, metadata, schema, link, asset, and package-boundary test results
Live governance, role, sensitive-access, or security evidence for external claims
Rendered review and source equality for changed promotional assets
passCriteria: Documented commands, outputs, supported boundaries, and limitations match current behavior
Security, governance, compliance, and support claims cite repository or external-control evidence
Every retained Markdown document is indexed and every shipped relative link resolves in the package
Changed assets are generated, accessible, visually reviewed, and not accepted through weaker thresholds

cadence.normal: Every full RHR; static documentation and asset checks on every relevant pull request
triggers: Public behavior, support boundary, governance, maintainer role, security claim, or release process changes
README, user documentation, package metadata, promotional messaging, or visual asset changes
Broken-link, misleading-copy, stale-evidence, accessibility, or continuity reports

continuousEvidence: Documentation, metadata, schema, link, package, and asset policy tests
Generated asset equality and render workflows
DCO and governance invariants in required CI
manualEvidence: Visual, accessibility, role, continuity, and live-control review

domains.title: Independent final verification
domains.objective: Independently verify the final review baseline from a clean checkout, distrust earlier summaries until rechecked, and assign every remaining issue a health-impacting disposition.
scope: Clean checkout, tracked inventory, integrated review range, worktree state, and final commit identity
Static quality, runtime tests, coverage, lifecycle, shell, migration, performance, and negative paths
Exact package artifact, clean installation, documentation, assets, security, and release controls
Required GitHub checks, open findings, risk acceptances, deferrals, and final health rationale
procedure: Start from a new clean checkout at the proposed final commit and inventory tracked files
Review integrated RHR changes and findings for regressions, contradictions, duplication, or stale evidence
Run the complete locally available verification and exact-artifact lifecycle suite
Read required CI and live-control results and classify every unavailable lane honestly
Set green, amber, or red independently from completion and record the rationale
requiredEvidence: Clean checkout path, final full SHA, tracked inventory, and clean worktree proof
Exact commands, versions, results, run URLs, artifacts, and hashes
Independent classification of every open finding and unavailable environment
Final health color, rationale, completion date, and revisit trigger
passCriteria: Every prior domain is complete and every finding has an owner and disposition
The final commit passes applicable local, hosted, artifact, documentation, security, and live-control checks
No Critical or High blocker is hidden by a completion decision
The parent can close with green, amber, or red because completion and health are recorded separately

cadence.normal: At the end of every full RHR; after all scoped domains for a partial RHR
triggers: Completion of a full RHR or release-readiness assessment
Material integrated change spanning multiple domains or security boundaries
Evidence that earlier domain reports may be stale, incomplete, or mutually inconsistent

continuousEvidence: Complete required CI graph
Full local test, coverage, lifecycle, package, documentation, and asset gates
Release and security negative-path tests
manualEvidence: Independent clean-checkout review and integrated finding classification
Final health judgment, rationale, and revisit decision
